comments.author: @WhereonEarth4K
comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Mali episode. Books, history, music, cultural resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE MALI COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, recording, and cultural item we'd recommend for this episode → https://amzn.to/4uAG4FV

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN MALI*
  ◦ Cultural and heritage experiences in Mali → https://shorturl.at/MmBBa
  ◦ Browse all Mali experiences → https://gyg.me/pTc7T0OC

*📖 PLAN YOUR MALI TRIP*
  ◦ Mali: The Bradt Travel Guide by Ross Velton → https://amzn.to/43yDbcU

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO MALI*
  ◦ The Bad-Ass Librarians of Timbuktu by Joshua Hammer → https://amzn.to/49VtBV2
  ◦ Born in Blackness by Howard W. French → https://amzn.to/4vdA9Xd
  ◦ Segu by Maryse Condé → https://amzn.to/4uDMbcP

*🍲 BRING MALI HOME*
  ◦ Simply West African by Pierre Thiam → https://amzn.to/4fLu53K
  ◦ Authentic African Bogolan Mud Cloth, handwoven in Mali → https://amzn.to/4f6itbr

replies.author: @tsa7122
replies.text: The word “dark” in reference to the Dark Ages refers to the stagnation of most cultures during this time. When the Roman Empire fell, no equivalent advanced culture like the Egyptians, Minoans, Etruscans, or Greeks was ready to fill the cultural vacuum left by the Romans.

comments.author: @lilianebarlow1719
comments.text: I had not heard the story of the manuscripts being taken out before the extremists arrived. A swedish academic had been cataloguing the colloction when Mali was attacked. The documentary told through her eyes suggested great losses occurred. It is critical that such cultural treasures are digitized. Yes, the original may be destroyed, and with it the important history of the actual books, but at least the knowledge  contained in them remains for future generations.

replies.author: @entredONG
replies.text:
​@TandenloosGreat but you'd need the Map of the transaharian trade routes network 
The first segment of the network ( walata_sijilmassa ) was built in 375 bce 
The last segment ( Cairo_Medina_Mecca ) was built in  300 AD 
The transaharian trade routes were very strategic for world economy half of the world Gold production came from the Ghana empire 
98 % of the world salt production also came from there .
These 2 commodities were the most important assets for world economy
